notes: teacher baseline; TensorBoard live; selector uses mean_distance when success_rate is below 0.01
best by mean_pregrasp_distance; success threshold still strict
best selected by balanced pregrasp distance and alignment; top-down folded home baseline
Arm-like gripper mount; random-target timed reach demo baseline.
45-degree deterministic blue pregrasp waypoint; reward improved but success/pregrasp success stayed zero; alignment learned negative direction.
Direction fixed: alignment became positive, but distance/pregrasp success remained poor; useful classroom reward-shaping failure case.
Short gripper improved pregrasp distance and produced occasional pregrasp success, but insertion_alignment stayed negative; suggests alignment should come before pregrasp contact.
Swapped stage order: insertion alignment first, then pregrasp. Insertion alignment became strongly positive and stage2 readiness appeared; stage3/final success remains sparse.

Teacher/Codex separated decision log added. Stage3 gate and classroom success band softened after alignment-first improved insertion direction; best checkpoint favors stage3 readiness with low contact penalty.
Full baseline after stage3 softening. Stage3 readiness became high and contact penalty stayed zero at selected checkpoint; final success remains sparse, so next bottleneck is touch-depth precision.

Added touch-depth reward and stage3_touch_ready metric. Result: stage3 path readiness stayed high and success improved slightly, but touch-depth remains sparse; next step should change curriculum/target geometry rather than only increasing scalar reward.
Resume from touch-depth best checkpoint; Stage-B narrows targets and emphasizes final insertion depth.
Stage-B replay reset starts from collected pregrasp states; highest success appears at replay-initial checkpoint, while later training lowers touch error but success remains sparse.
Stage 2 uses the midpoint between gripper pads as the pregrasp point. Stage1 alignment is high and gripper-center pregrasp distance improved, but final insertion success remains low.
Pregrasp entry->center->insertion curriculum with replay reset. Best checkpoint reached high stage3 insertion readiness, but final touch/success remains low.

GUI visual run from model_600 with final center capture reward. Stage3 insertion/touch became strong; stage4 final center success appeared but remained sparse, so next work should use a stage4-specific replay curriculum or slightly relaxed final radius.

Stage4 replay reset with smaller red target radius 0.025m and relaxed final center radius 0.035m. Stage3 touch improved strongly and target contact penalty stayed zero, but stage4 center success remained sparse; next step should reduce action std/exploration or collect states closer to the red center.
Stage4 center-push reward: progress from pregrasp target toward red target center while maintaining insertion alignment and lateral accuracy. Best checkpoint selected before late drift.

Stronger Stage4 push curriculum: reward new best center-push progress and deeper push after progress 0.5. Improved push progress and produced sparse final center success, but final success remains low.

Stage4 diversified reward: distance improvement is scaled by shortest-path score, and red-target distance shells pay only when the policy reaches a closer shell. Improved stage3/touch stability and path score, but push-ready remains low.

Stage4 time pressure added until terminal success; success step returns success bonus only. Slightly raised exploration/action scale. Best model_1600: stage3_touch 0.786, stage4_push 0.199, push_progress 0.605, shortest_path 0.673, stage4_time 0.413, success still sparse.

Relaxed final center success radius from 0.035m to 0.055m to create a measurable success signal before tightening. Best model_1600 reached success_rate 0.0286 and stage4_center 0.0413, but stage3/pregrasp stability dropped, so next step should tighten gradually or preserve stage3 gates.

Tightened relaxed final center radius from 0.055m to 0.045m. Best model_1600 kept meaningful success_rate 0.0144 while improving stage3_touch to 0.3669 versus the 0.055m run. Later iterations improved stage3 but lost final success, suggesting stage-weight/time-weight separation is the next candidate.

Added small time-ramped stage3 preserve reward, terminal-only success quality bonus, higher success bonus, and slightly stronger stage4 time penalty. Best model_1600 kept success_rate 0.0132 and slightly improved stage3_touch to 0.3716 versus relaxed045. Later iterations preserve stage3 much better but still lose final center success, so next step should strengthen terminal transition/push rather than stage3 preservation.
near-terminal bridge reward added; success stayed near weighted baseline, stage3 held, contact penalty remained zero; next candidate is stage latch plus progressive stage weighting

Teacher idea: latch each completed stage and increase preserved-stage weight after later progress. Codex proposal: keep the weight modest and measure whether stage3 is preserved without losing stage4 center. Result: latch metrics improved strongly, target contact stayed zero, but final center success remained sparse, so next experiment should shift weight from preservation to final insertion after stage3 latch.

Teacher idea: move the blue pregrasp marker toward the red target in two or three steps after alignment/reach. Codex proposal: cap the moving blue guide at 70 percent of the safe touch path, then enable final-insertion reward only after the guided stages. Result: moving guide worked and stage4 push improved by the final checkpoint, but strict final center success still selected the initial checkpoint; next step should use a guided-progress checkpoint for play or relax/tune the final center condition.

Teacher idea: move final blue marker to the red sphere center and treat following blue as the final success path. Codex proposal: add MOVING_PREGRASP_GOAL=center, set desired touch distance to zero, and keep non-gripper target contact penalty active. Result: guided blue path worked well (moving_pregrasp_final_rate best 0.8420), safety stayed clean (target_contact_penalty 0.0), but strict center success remained low (~0.001), so next step should relax/shape final center success or train a dedicated terminal-center phase.

Teacher observation: old play used an existing model and blue marker vanished too quickly, often touching with one gripper finger instead of centering. Codex adjustment: train with smaller visual blue marker, 5 blue guide steps, 0.035m step radius, and 4-frame hold gate before each marker advance. Result: first too-strict 0.018m/8-frame attempt was stopped because moving_pregrasp_final_rate stayed near zero. Relaxed run produced moving guide signal; best checkpoint model_2100 has moving_pregrasp_final_rate 0.019 and stage3_latched 0.892, while live mid-run peaks reached about 0.10 moving final. Strict success is still low, so next step is to increase guided-step reward or use a two-phase schedule before tightening radius.

Teacher idea: use the blue guide as a fan/funnel approach region rather than one exact touch point. Codex implementation: added moving_pregrasp_funnel reward and broad 800-iteration curriculum. Result: moving pregrasp improved strongly, but strict final insertion remained near zero; next step is a separate final insertion curriculum.

Teacher idea: use one-shot distance shells and exponential reward for the blue guide, and count final blue-center readiness as success. Result: new exp/shell metrics are active and success_rate reached 0.00146 at selected checkpoint, but moving_pregrasp_final_rate stayed very low, so the current 0.035m/4-frame gate is still too strict for 300 iterations.
